task_completion.description: Access the OpenC3 COSMOS web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the OpenC3 COSMOS interface.
The Timeline application within OpenC3 COSMOS is visible, showing a created timeline explicitly named 'MAINT_SCHEDULE'.

task_completion.description: Schedule a maintenance activity at least 30 minutes in the future
task_completion.visual_evidence: An activity block or entry is visible in the 'MAINT_SCHEDULE' timeline. The scheduled start time must be at least 30 minutes later than the current system time shown in the OS taskbar, and the activity description must mention maintenance.

task_completion.description: Include a command sequence with at least two INST commands
task_completion.visual_evidence: The details or configuration of the scheduled activity show a sequence of commands, with at least two commands targeting 'INST' (e.g., INST COLLECT, INST STATUS).

task_completion.description: Document the scheduled activity in a JSON report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displays the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/timeline_schedule.json'. The file must contain valid JSON with the keys 'timeline_name', 'activity_start_time', 'commands', and 'activity_description'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenC3 COSMOS GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating and clicking through the OpenC3 COSMOS web interface to create the timeline and schedule the activity, rather than exclusively using terminal scripts or API calls.

integrity.description: JSON report data matches the COSMOS GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The values for start time, description, and commands in the JSON report exactly match the configuration visible in the OpenC3 COSMOS Timeline interface.
The agent is seen writing the JSON file using a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) or terminal commands (like echo or cat), rather than just claiming the file was created.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The timeline must be exactly named 'MAINT_SCHEDULE'. The output JSON report must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/timeline_schedule.json' and must include the specific keys: 'timeline_name', 'activity_start_time', 'commands', and 'activity_description'. The agent assumes the role of a Payload Operations Engineer.
